entry.title: Back can't be used as a parameter name
entry.content:
Try this little report.
 
REPORT.
 
PARAMETERS back.
 
 
Syntactically incorrect!
 
Incorrect nesting: For the statement "ENDMODULE", there is no open structure introduced by "MODULE".  
Module %_BACK is already defined as a INPUT module.  
 
 
I've never seen this before. Seems we can't use back as a parameter name.

Hi @NaveenR - I am not sure if a recursive way is possible. But perhaps you need a series of time valuatiions which checks these increments (like how we do lookup of seniority for all ranges), you need a time valuation for each such possible range or increment. This can get humungous

The other alternative is to check if you can have this covered using business rules in time val using multiple if else and ranges. I have not explored the business rules yet for this purpose but you can give it a try

entry.title: Scheduling Stories - Dynamic Time Filters Issue
entry.content:
We would like to be able to use dynamic time filters in Stories for running and scheduling reports - but they are not in line with currently available scheduling filters.

Filters like "Previous Month" work fine when we run the reports, but they fail when we schedule them, because the As of Date options for Time Filters in scheduling allow only for a calendar date - so when we schedule a report as of August 1st, the Previous Month dynamic filter always shows July, no matter whether the schedule runs in August or September.

Will it be possible to schedule Stories with dynamic as of date filter? At least to schedule them as of Today?

Aga Czoska, Kennametal

entry.title: How to set environment variables without overwriting existing values with MTA in BTP (CF)?
entry.content:
Hey everyone,

TLDR: How can I set an environment variable with my MTA without overwriting the variables value if it already exists?

Context: We use the Telemetry Plugin in our CAP application. This way we can monitor our application with the Cloud Logging service in BTP.

We do not want to enable telemetry for all our systems. In order to disable the telemetry plugin in a system, NO_TELEMETRY has to be true. In BTP, this has to be done in the "User-provided Variables" in the CF application. 

I set the "User-provided variable" in our MTA config as explained here, with:

 

...
modules:
  - name: app-name
  ...
  properties:
    NO_TELEMETRY: "true"
...

 

 It does what it should and sets the variable for the app in BTP. 

The problem now is that I do not want to overwrite existing values. When I enable the telemetry plugin for an app by manually changing NO_TELEMETRY to false, I don't want the next deployment to overwrite and reset the value to true.

Does anyone know how I can achieve this?

Thanks in advance and best regards.

Robin

entry.title: Re: Name of Statuses displayed by the Curricula Complianc...
entry.content:
Hi @barryelissa 

in the learning administration under "References" -> "Manage Labels" -> "Labels" you can add a filter for "Label Value" and search for the value you wann to change. 

Searaching for "Curriculum Compliance Expired" showed my label "label.CurriculumComplianceExpired" that you can edit in the "Locales" tab of the label. 

Curriculum Compliance Ending: label.CurriculumComplianceEnding 
Curriculum Compliance Current: label.CurriculumComplianceCurrent

Tip: Keep those labels on a list to check after each Update to make sure SAP did not change this specific wording with the update. 

Cheers, 
Marc

entry.title: Re: SAP to NON SAP connection
entry.content:
The answer is "yes".

However, describing the procedure is impossible. You did not provide enough details for that: what kind of interface do Oracle and Informatica provide? ODBC/JDBC-based? WebService, OData, SOAP, REST or one of those HTTP-based interfaces? A direct programming interface (C, Java, etc.) so that an RFC-based library can be plugged into the third-party application? Or does the application perhaps provide a client library, which could be plugged into an SAP middleware?

You need to do some research into which kind of interface these third-party applications provide, and then we could describe, how it can be mapped/converted to an SAP interface. If the application does not provide one of the "standard" interfaces, you may need a middleware like SAP Business Connector in addition to the Cloud Connector, which can then "translate" e.g. a proprietary Informatica protocol/data format to a protocol/data format understood by the SAP system.

entry.title: SAP Business Network for Logistics 2408 and 2409 Releases – What’s New?
entry.content:
We are back after the summer and want to share the highlights of our 2408 and 2409 releases with you!

As usual, this blog is designed for those seeking a detailed overview of the enhancements launched for SAP Business Network for Logistics. 

Over the past two months, some very exciting features have been released, amongst them the integration scenario between SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration and SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration to improve supply chain resiliency with end-to-end shipment visibility. In an inbound transportation scenario, buyers can now initiate transportation planning by sharing the advanced ship notice from their supplier with their logistics service providers and receive tracking information.

  We are also happy to share that two requests from influence portal have been released:

  • Carriers can now view additional business partner details such as shipping party, consignee party, executing shipper party and invoicing party in their contracting applications in SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration.
  • SAP Business Network, intelligent insights add-on now supports the recreation of a tracked process for analytic purposes.

The three features mentioned above as well as other release highlights are detailed out below.

 

Integration between SAP Business Network Supply Chain Collaboration and SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Buyer managed inbound transportation with logistics service provider

Through this new capability, buyers can now initiate the transportation planning by sharing the advanced ship notice from their suppliers with their logistics service providers. The logistics service provider shares the planned shipment with the buyer along with updates to the advanced ship notice.

 

 In this way, buyers automatically get updates when their purchase is scheduled to arrive including updates on potential changes to the arrival date. Buyers can plan their warehousing, production, and customer expectations more accurately.

Find more details on this scenario in the feature detail deck or watch a demo.

 

Cross SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration and SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

New event type ‘exception resolved’

The "Consumer API for Order Tracking (FC)", "Provider API for Order Events (FC)", "Consumer API for Order Tracking (GTT)", and "Provider API for Order Events (GTT)" now support the event type ‘exception resolved’.

It indicates the exception in the logistics process has been handled.

In SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace, the event can be displayed in the events table and tracking timeline of the following fulfillment tracking apps:

  • “Track Shipments”
  • “Monitor Inbound ASNs”
  • “Monitor Outbound Deliveries”

Adding notes in event reporting

Carriers and data contributors can now add notes when they report an event. In the “Provider API for Order Events (GTT)” and “Provider API for Order Events (FC)”, the note field was added to the event object. It allows you to provide additional information when reporting events.

Host URL now displayed in the “System Connections” app

In the “System Connections” app under ‘Connection Settings’, the Host URL field is now available under Service Credentials. You can copy the host URL and use it when you configure the Provider API endpoints for inbound scenarios. For more information, see Enabling Inbound Communication.

 Error categorization and automatic reprocessing of failed messages

We have enabled automatic reprocessing of integration messages for selected error codes as follows:

  • We now check on an hourly basis if failed and escalated messages can be reprocessed automatically, and we start the reprocessing accordingly.
  • We now automatically reprocess failed and escalated messages after a downtime.

 

SAP Business Network Freight Collaboration

Send reference documents to external system as notes

In the “General Settings” app, in the ‘Appointment and Dispute Settings’ tab, shippers can now maintain the settings for reference documents in the ‘Settlement’ section after having selected an ordering party. Shippers can also specify whether they want to send reference documents to external system as notes.

 Contracting: view additional business partner details in “Freight Order for Confirmation” and “Freight Requests for Quotation” applications

We were following requests from the influence portal and carriers can now view additional business partner details under the tab ‘Contacts’ in the “Freight Order for Confirmation” and “Freight Requests for Quotation” applications.

 

Contracting: view business partner details in the “Confirm Freight Bookings” (Air and Ocean) applications

Carriers can now view details on executing carrier party and invoicing party in the “Confirm Freight Bookings Ocean” and “Confirm Freight Bookings (Air)” applications under the tab ‘Contacts’.

Dock appointment scheduling: flexible time slot management for dock appointments

You can now create a flexible time slot duration for a loading point on loading point, validity period and working hours level.

The time slot duration was changed from up to 6 hours to between 5 minutes and 23:59 hours.

Please note: the field label was changed from ‘Time Slot’ to ‘Time Slot (duration)’. Therefore, in the Microsoft Excel file used for editing the time slot duration on loading point level, you must now use the following heading: ‘Time Slot (duration)’.

Settlement: create custom charge codes

SAP provides a list of standard charge codes. These are documented and published on SAP Help Portal. Customers might be using multiple charge codes for a similar meaning for which SAP provides one standard charge code.

The “Assign Charge Code” app allows a n:1 mapping. This results in an error when carriers try to enter values for such codes and an error message is being processed. This can also be seen in the “Manage Integration Logs” app.

 In the "Assign Charge Codes" app, shippers can now create custom codes and use them for charge type mapping.

Carriers can use the "Provider API for Invoicing” to submit invoices based on the custom codes created by the ordering party.

Settlement: Enhanced validations for inbound API

When a carrier submits an invoice via “Provider API for Invoicing”, there are the following new validations:

  • Active invoice may already exist for the selected freight document or invoice may have been submitted to the shipper for review
  • An attachment with the same name and file type exists already
  • Charge type cannot be invoiced as an unplanned charge because the shipper has not enabled the necessary settings

In case any of the above validations is detected, an error message is thrown.

Settlement: add and view reference documents

In the “Manage Invoices” app on the details page, on the tab ‘Information’, a section for reference documents has been added. Carriers can add reference documents here and view existing ones. When creating an invoice using the "Provider API for Invoicing", carriers can also submit reference documents along with the invoice.

Please note: the documents are available throughout all settlement apps and are carried through the entire process. You can for example also view them in the “Manage Disputes” application – again under the tab ‘Information’.

 

SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace

Code list enhancements to add carrier descriptions

The following enhancements were made to code lists:

  • A new standard code list ‘CarrierDescription’ was added to define carrier names in the “Manage Models” application.
  • The code names can be displayed as the carrier name in the “Track Shipments” application.
  • You can also filter shipments by carrier name.

This converts previously technical IDs into readable and searchable carrier names.

New attachment formats TIFF supported

In the events table and the tracking timeline, you can now upload TIFF files as attachments when reporting events. You can also download attachments of this file type. Both file name extensions are supported: .tif and .tiff.

Note field added to event detail page

In fulfillment tracking apps, the 'Note' field is added to the event details page to view the additional information of the event reported by visibility providers or carriers.

Notes from events can be synchronized back to SAP Transportation Management

When reporting events, the note information can be synchronized to SAP Transportation Management. For more, see Event to Action External Functions in the Guide for Model Administrators.

“Track Shipments” application: map enhancement

On the shipment details and freight unit detail pages, the map is enhanced to support greater number of events. It offers more information and allows you to drill down to the tracking unit level. In the enhanced map, you’ll have the following two views:

  • An overview of current positions of all tracking units in a shipment or a freight unit and their status.
  • A detailed view where you can view the real-time transportation routes of an individual tracking unit.

There is also a short video on this new feature: https://video.sap.com/media/t/1_kt2nw2x0

The enhanced map is only displayed for shipments and freight units that meet the following two prerequisites:

  • The shipment or freight unit is assigned with at least one tracking unit.
  • The total number of actual events in the shipment or freight unit exceeds 10,000.

Otherwise, the old map is displayed. For more, see Guide for Transportation Planners.

“Track Shipments” application: filter/display by departure and arrival country/region

On the worklist page, solution owners can now filter shipments by its departure country/region and arrival country/region and see the information in the corresponding columns of the worklist.

 Note: In addition, you can use the ‘Adapt UI’ functionality to display departure and arrival country in the header section or the ‘Information’ tab on the shipment detail page.

Sunset of resource tracking services

All resource tracking related services have been sunset:

  • Resource Road Tracking FTL
  • Resource Road Tracking LTL
  • Resource Ocean Tracking FCL
  • Resource Ocean Tracking LCL
  • Resource Tracking All
  • Resource Tracking Parcel

You can use the shipment tracking related services instead:

  • Shipment Road Tracking LTL
  • Shipment Tracking All
  • Shipment Road Tracking FTL
  • Shipment Ocean Tracking FCL
  • Shipment Ocean Tracking LCL
  • Shipment Air Tracking All
  • Shipment Tracking Parcel
  • Shipment Rail Tracking All

Deprecation of “Create Shipment Advice” application

The “Create Shipment Advice” app is deprecated as of September 21, 2024. We plan to remove the app from the data contributor’s Fiori Launchpad as of March 15, 2025.

 

SAP Business Network, intelligent insights add-on

SAP Business Network, intelligent insights add-on now on US20

The SAP Business Network, intelligent insights add-on is now also hosted on Microsoft Azure data center for US West (US20). Please note: the integration with Everstream is not supported on this data center.

Recreated tracked process supported

When you delete a tracked process in SAP Business Network Global Track and Trace, the relevant data was also deleted in the intelligent insights add-on as well.

Upon customer requests from influence portal, now if you recreate the same tracked process, it can still be updated to the intelligent insights add-on and is available for dashboard analysis and real-time monitor in the “Logistics Execution Insights” app and the “Manage Analytic Stories” app.

Personalization of shipment list

In “Logistics Execution Insights” application, you can sort the shipment list according to your needs. Now, you can save your personalized view and export it to the home screen.

 

 

Please share and amplify our new features!

---------------------

The above summarizes the key innovations for 2408 and 2409. We hope you enjoy the new capabilities. Thanks for reading the blog post and stay tuned! 

A full overview of the 2408 and 2409 releases can be found in the following link.

To learn more about SAP Business Network for Logistics visit SAP - Business Network for Logistics.

entry.title: Can the onboarding data of a future employee be transferred to SAP via the standard interface PA Rep
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

is there a way for the onboarding personnel data of a future employee to be transferred to SAP ERP via the standard interface PA Replication SF->SAP HCM ERP/HANA before the future start date? This is necessary for certain company-specific onboarding processes that are based on the future employee's master data. It is necessary to make settings for the employee before entering the system so that they have all work tools and data available on the first day. Certain onboarding activities take place in SF Onboarding, others in SAP ERP.

The aim is to transfer the person and the assignment to the OM structure before the actual entry into SAP ERP.

I would be very happy to hear ideas and possible solutions.

Regards
Wiebke

entry.title: Re: SAP future strategy around Scope Items BH1, BH2 & BJ2 in Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Hi Riyaj,

Currently there are no plans to deprecate Best Practices BH1, BH2 and BJ2 from Private Cloud. Customers are allowed to utilize these best practices, but we highly recommend adopting new best practices 4HH and 4HI. Most of the innovation will take place in these new best practices.

Regards, Sanjeev

entry.title: Re: SAP Business Application Studio doesn't connect to our On-Premise System / AxiosError 403
entry.content:
Hi Kevin,

Few points I recommend you to verify:

1. Make sure that the destination URL (including port) matches exactly the virtual URL as defined in your cloud connector. If using HTTPS to access your system, make sure you use the system's https port in both internal and virtual (and destination) URLs.

2. Make sure you allow also sub-paths for the paths you defined for your system in the cloud connector. Specifically the OData services exploration requires access to sub-paths of /sap/opu/odata, but runtime of your developed application will require other sub-paths as well.

3. Try to access from your internal network through your browser directly to the system (with the internal URL defined in cloud connector, and credentials provided in the destination), for example to this request which is used for discovering OData V2 services in your system: <internal-system-url>/sap/opu/odata/IWFND/CATALOGSERVICE;v=2/ServiceCollection

4. I suggest you to also use the following troubleshooting guide: https://gad5158842f.us2.hana.ondemand.com/dtp/viewer/#/tree/2827/actions/41344:41346:57775/?version=current

Thanks,
Rotem

entry.title: Re: SD downpayment amount should include tax value
entry.content:
I have solved this issue by myself. Just make some notes for me and others who ran into this same situation for reference.

1. this requirement is not really want to post tax in FAZ type billing, because as to local tax rule, no real sales-tax need to post. the real objective is to make the partial billing/ down-payment request to customer by a gross amount( portion of contract TOTAL amount ) 

2.it seems on billing plan, it ALWAY calculate down-payment amount in net value, not Gross value. so, I have to make a approach to add the tax part amount into FAZ billing.

3.what I do as following

3.1 make a customized condition type, and the condition class is E-Extra pay and Calculation type is A-Percentage, and it is manual entry

 

3.2 I add it to the after the total amount calculation, the detail is showing as below.

4. billing process

when generating down-payment request, I add the Z005 in pricing from header. it transfer the extra pay to item condition

it can be posted into note document

 then, you can contione to clear the down-payment request after receving customer down-payment with F-29

entry.title: Re: Readiness Check: Maintenance Planner Error
entry.content:
Hello Kaiwen, 
we do have a KBA out there which gives hints and explanations about the steps to be carried out in order to check further and to solve the issue you are facing. The KBA is 2847830 - "SAP Readiness Check 2.0 "Add-On Compatibility" and "Active Business Functions" tiles are empty".
For this particular check in Readiness Check we are using SAP's maintenance planner information for Active Business Functions and Add-Ons. This data is expected to get updated in Maintenance Planner automatically by at least one of the following data sources: LMDB (Landscape Management Database) of SAP Solution Manager, LMDB of SAP Focused Run, direct data sending from the systems itself or SAP EarlyWatch Alert.

2408453 - "Synchronization of system data with the SAP Support Portal in Solution Manager 7.2 SP05 and later"
2887157 - "System Upload from SAP Focused Run to SAP Support Portal"
2855951 - "How to Configure SLD Data Supplier to Directly Send Data to SAP's Support Backbone"
3498585 - SAP EarlyWatch Alert as Data Source for Maintenance Planner and System Data

Best regards,

Romy

entry.title: Re: 999 – The Number of the BSEG
entry.content:
Well, Iron Maiden, Lady Gaga, Monty Python...whatever works! (that's the title of a quite funny Woody Allen movie, by the way 😉
Thanks for the kudos!
Regarding your experiences: that was exactly my aim, to cut through all these opinions and (often contradicting) recommendations you'll find all over the different sources, and provide both a theoretical/architectural background and a practical solution. Also, don't get me wrong: the automatic document split was definitely an enormous step forward (as a rescue) back in the day, but in S/4HANA the summarization is just the better option. Because it makes it clear that we just don't need all those detailed non-operational BSEG items any more, we have the details in the ACDOCA; it follows and emphasizes the architectural distinction between FIN Operational and FIN Accounting; and it reduces the HANA footprint, too.
BSTAT 'U' and BAPIs: well, sure, if you just set the BSTAT to 'U' in the BAPI and provide AP/AR or tax relevant or OI managed items, the system will mercilessly return an error "you're not allowed to post BSTAT U". This will only work with pure G/L postings.
What you could do is feed a '1' into the BAPI header field EV_POSTNG_CTRL (this means "try to suppress the BSEG update", please see note 2591291 mentioned above), and additionally use the BAdi BADI_FINS_SUMMARIZE_BSEG in order to summarize the BSEGs to the max. Then you leave it up to the system to check if the BSEG update can be suppressed, and if not, summarize it to the max.

Kind regards,
Stefan

entry.title: CustomData attribute (data-) not working as documented on XML
entry.content:
Hello,

I'm finding some trouble in SAPUI5 to get `data-` attributes written to DOM. I need a simple implementation as I will need to manually add in several XML elements this data attribute

I'm aware that I could use `<CustomData />`, but the perfect implementation for my need is to create a new XML namespace for http://schemas.sap.com/sapui5/extension/sap.ui.core.CustomData/1 and then evoke it on the element, as in:

<Button namespace:testid="foobar" />

 The problem is that it's not rendering on DOM and I just can't find any more ways to tackle this on the documentation, and even here.

This is a short example of my implementation, and the problem: https://jsfiddle.net/kpvnw760/4/

If you notice, once you run the code linked above, and if you do:

document.querySelector("button").dataset

It will only list "sapUI" and "ui5Accesskey", never "testId" nor "foobar".

The documentation reference I'm using is: https://ui5.sap.com/#/topic/91f0c3ee6f4d1014b6dd926db0e91070 (section: "Use in XML Views").

 

Thank you a lot, in advance!

entry.title: ZDM DB Upgrades and CAP Multitenancy
entry.content:
We have a multitenant app and currently as suggested in the CAP documentation we use blue-green deploy to upgrade all tenants using the cds-mtx upgradetask on idle start of the sidecar. 

hooks:
  - name: upgrade-all
    type: task
    phases:
      - blue-green.application.before-start.idle
    parameters:
      name: upgrade
      memory: 512M
      disk-quota: 768M
      command: cds-mtx upgrade '*'

CAP docs -> multitenancy -> update-database-schema 

It's often desired to update the whole service in a zero downtime manner. This section doesn't deal with the details about updating a service productively, but describes tool support the CAP Java SDK offers to update tenants.

CAP docs -> Java -> multitenancy -> database update 

We want to have more control over the DB upgrade process

for example, what happens if something goes wrong?  

Using blue green will stop the idle service from becoming live if one of the tenant DB upgrades fails, but that is a partial win, as not using Blue Green the sidecar is stopped.

We want to investigate something like the legacy blue-green CF MTA Deploy had, where we could use the Zero Downtime Maintenance (ZDM) HDI deployer, we tried to use this with the sidecar without success.

The advantage with ZDM Deployment similar to Blue Green it handles updates gracefully by creating a new container and treating access and data changes separately only moving only making the new container live on successful deploy.

Zero-Downtime Maintenance with Multi-Target Applications

Here is what we tried

  # --------------------- SIDECAR MODULE -----------------------
  - name: killerapp-mtx-sidecar
    # ----------------------------------------------------------
    type: nodejs
    path: mtx/sidecar
    requires:
      - name: app-api
        properties:
          SUBSCRIPTION_URL: ~{app-protocol}://\${tenant_subdomain}-~{app-uri}
          HDI_DEPLOY_MODE: ZDM   
          HDI_DEPLOY_OPTIONS: { "verbose": true, "trace": true }

HDI_DEPLOY_OPTIONS are mapped ok

HDI_DEPLOY_MODE: ZDM is ignored

the sidecar code cds-mtxs -> srv -> plugins -> hana looks like HDI_DEPLOY_MODE is deleted and not mapped back.

is the ZDMDeployer not supported?

how are other people ensuring zero downtime, what is the best practice here?

entry.title: Re: How I can change my company ANID after Trading Relati...
entry.content:
Hi,

Once the customer has sent you the PO to a certain ANID and you have accepted that PO via a certain ANID you cannot change it yourself to the right ANID.  But you can go to your account where the relationship was accepted, under 'Settings' 'Customer relationships' then select the customer and choose 'reject'.  Next step is to contact your customer to resend the PO to your ANID of choice so you can log in via your correct ANID and then respond to the PO there. 

You can find more help on this in our page SAP Business Network for Suppliers | SAP Community, I recommend the Getting Started section and Learning resources:  in particular our Supplier learning Portal (see video there on 'Connect with your Customer (TRR invite)' or our 'SAP Business Network Administration Process Guide'.

For future reference you can select the community SAP managed tag 'SAP Business Network for Suppliers' for supplier related questions.  I hope this is helpful

entry.title: Parallel Accounting and Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S4 HANA Finance
entry.content:
Parallel Accounting and Universal Parallel Accounting in SAP S4 HANA Finance

Vol. 1 Parallel Accounting

What is Parallel Accounting?

There are different accounting standards that ensure accuracy and transparency for the organization. It allows fundamental frameworks, policies, and rules to report the financial transaction. 

While business operates into a broader scope in terms of geopolitical phenomena, it requires or has an organizational mandate to follow compliance in the global as well as regional environment in terms of attending accountability and reporting transparency. Moreover, legal compliance creates a crucial role to accord with more than one accounting standard. Here parallel accounting comes into play to comply with transparency, regional competency, and accountability into the reporting standard.

Parallel accounting is one of the crucial subsets of Universal Journal that allows multidimensional reporting and allows integrated financial data reporting in a single chart of accounts.

Parallel accounting enables maintaining multiple accounting standards, like US GAAP and IFRS, based on business requirements and compliance mandates. It allows organizations to propose the financial report on different accounting standards and financial statement versions that cater to different regulatory requirements, industry standards, and internal reporting needs.

Why Parallel Accounting?

Financial reporting is crucial to organizational transparency and accountability. Moreover, it reflects financial performances to the external and internal stakeholders. Hence, maintaining relevant accounting standards for financial reports is crucial to ensuring financial accountability across the globe.

Now it is very important to accommodate or enable parallel accounting into SAP that complies with complex characteristics of financial components. Let’s take an example of incurring research and development expenses for innovation. According to the US GAAP, research and development expenses are treated as operating expenses that would reflect into the P/L statement as expenses. On the other hand, IFRS considers research and development expenses as assets that would come under the asset side on the balance sheet. Now the question is how to report the financial statement for “Research and Development,” while organizations need to comply with both US GAAP and IFRS. Here, parallel accounting enables the solution for the above question. It designed the functionality of the recording; the transaction has different characteristics based upon different accounting standards.

Scope of Parallel Accounting

The scope of parallel accounting is wide and has been applied to the different application components. Below are the key application components where parallel accounting plays a crucial role.

  1. · General Ledger Accounting

Based on the business requirement, parallel accounting allows regrouping and sorting of receivables and payables for different accounting principles. Parallel accounting also enables dynamic value adjustments for doubtful receivables. Moreover, parallel accounting intervenes in foreign currency valuation and allows posting for adjusted values of transactions posted in foreign currency.

  1. · Asset accounting

SAP allows to define different depreciation areas based on the different accounting principles. By assigning types of valuation or accounting principles to depreciation areas, SAP allows the integration between parallel valuations based on different accounting principles in asset accounting. There are two different ways to integrate asset accounting with parallel valuation; one is the ledger approach, and the other is the account approach. In the ledger approach, accounting principles are assigned to separate ledgers, which allows for the posting of separate documents for each accounting principle or valuation. In the account approach, different accounts are being created that represent different valuations for asset accounting. Likewise, in the ledger approach, separate documents are posted for each accounting principle or valuation.

  1. · Treasury and Risk Management

There is a strong integration of parallel accounting with Treasury and risk management. In Treasury and Risk Management, systems allow to post accounting entries in different valuation areas in accordance with different accounting principles. Here, valuation areas portray distinct accounting principles. Valuation areas are assigned with accounting principles and allow to post values to different accounting principles.

Valuation areas offer different classification categories that can be used for valuation specifications for each accounting principle. Below are the classification categories used in Treasury and Risk Management.

Valuation classes allow to distinguish financial assets as per the holding categories, such as availability for sales or held-to-maturity for the IFRS statement. As assets are assigned to different valuation classes, financial transactions can be posted on the specific accounts based on the nature and characteristics of the transaction.

Differentiation helps to define the structure of the balance sheet accounts by using characteristics upon them.

Single-position management proposes the concept of lot accounting based on which different consumption sequence procedures can be applied for specific financial products.

Position management procedures enable control over balance sheet accounts with three key parameters: valuation classes, valuation areas, and other characteristics.

  1. · Parallel Accounting in Controlling

In Controlling, multiple valuation approaches can be displayed in accordance with different valuation accounting principles used in the business vertical. The system enables features to use parallel accounting by using additional accounts. Based on the present scenarios, businesses will decide the accounting principles used for controlling and, moreover, need to create cost elements and revenue elements for accounting principles. Position Management Procedures, a feature, enable prescribed valuation approaches that can be assigned to balance sheet accounts based on valuation areas, valuation class, and other relevant characteristics.

 

 

Benefits of Parallel Accounting

  • Enable multidimensional reporting and integrated financial data processing: Parallel accounting is one of the crucial subsets of Universal Journal that allows multidimensional reporting and allows integrated financial data reporting in a single chart of accounts.
  • Allows different financial statement versions: It allows organizations to propose the financial report on different accounting standards and financial statement versions that cater to different regulatory requirements, industry standards, and internal reporting needs. Moreover,
  • Parallel accounting and currencies: Parallel accounting enables using one local currency and an additional two parallel currencies to represent distinct valuations based on the currency. In a simple word, SAP allows 3 multi-dimensional currencies for each accounting principle. With multi-currency capabilities, businesses can process their valuation and key closing operations.
  • Provides consolidated views and alternative fiscal year variant approaches: Parallel accounting offers increased data transparency and real-time analytics through consolidated views that help in potential decision-making. Moreover, parallel accounting offers alternative fiscal year variant reports that provide comprehensive analytics and insights.
  • Removal of data redundancy: Parallel accounting enables diagnosed, effective, and sustainable reporting procedures that reduce data duplication and redundancy.

Approaches in SAP

There are two ways to maintain parallel accounting in SAP: the accounting approach and the ledger approach.

  1. Accounting Approach:

In the accounting approach, each general ledger account with the distinct characteristics gets created and aligned with responsible financial statement versions. Each chart of accounts is created based on accounting principles, and any number of financial statement versions can be assigned per chart of accounts. So, the account approach ecosystem contains different general ledger accounts, like adjustments accounts with different financial statement versions, and only one ledger. Separate documents are getting posted on each valuation or accounting principle.

Figure 1: Object flows in Accounting Approach

  1. Ledger Approach:

In the ledger approach, a separate ledger is being created in accordance with separate accounting principles (GAAP and IFRS). With this ledger approach, accounting entries should be posted to the specific ledger according to the nature of the transaction. However, accounting entries are getting posted into two ledgers at a single time, whether there are no specifications or indications of ledgers to be used. In the ledger approach ecosystem, there are only single financial versions with different ledgers. However, there is no requirement of creating adjustment accounts.

Figure 2: Object flows in Ledger approach

  1. Leading Ledger

Leading ledger is the default ledger that is assigned to all company codes and acts as a global ledger. In SAP S4 HANA, the leading ledger is referred to as 0L.

  1. Non-Leading Ledger

Non-leading can be referred to as a parallel ledger that represents the local accounting principles being used for reporting purposes. In SAP S4 HANA, non-leading ledger is marked as 2L.

 

Vol 2: Universal Parallel Accounting

 

Release

This concept is introduced in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition 2105 release, and delivered to SAP S/4HANA 2022 and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition customers in October 2022.

Background

Universal Parallel Accounting proposed a harmonized reporting architecture for ledgers and currencies that makes period-end reporting simpler and reduces complexity across the entire company accounting landscape. It enables parallel management valuations represented by additional currency types to distinguish a legal valuation from a group valuation.

There are some key challenges in traditional parallel accounting that lead to additional period-end closing activities, cumbersome filtering of values in reporting, and complex configuration. Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA) revokes those challenges and offers a synchronized and harmonized architecture for reporting and analytics. 

Features and benefits of Universal Parallel Accounting (UPA)

UPA has played a pivotal role in group valuation and multi-currency reporting. This section will demonstrate the key features and benefits from UPA that businesses can achieve in terms of smooth business operation and financial reporting. With unconsolidated views, Universal Parallel Accounting allows valuation of material based on different ledger prices and asserts production variances and contribution margins for each ledger. Alternative Fiscal Years allows multi-fiscal year capabilities per ledger and/or company code to overcome the boundaries of fiscal year variant complexities and additional period-end activities. Multicurrency capabilities support up to ten currencies to calculate and post in parallel. Moreover, parallel accounting supports settlement per ledger and introduces the concept of ledger-specific settlement rules. In addition to this, UPA supports group valuation with consolidated views approaches, which eliminate inter-company revenues and COGS.

UPA in inventory valuation

UPA proposes an extensive segmentation approach to inventory valuation in terms of ledger-specific valuation, which allows more granular representation of different accounting standards for standard costs, work-in-process, finished goods inventories, and actual cost of goods sold. UPA introduces the value approach with settlement per ledger, actual costing runs per ledger, and inventory price per ledger. An audit trail for standard and actual costing can be executed with enhanced transparency and accuracy.

UPA in Asset Accounting

Universal parallel accounting enables settlement runs for AuC to final asset per ledger, which leads to monitoring and tracking of the complete asset life cycle picture per ledger basis. It also helps to assert actual asset values per accounting standard at the ledger level.

UPA in Overhead accounting

Previously, in General Ledger Accounting only the values in the leading ledger were transferred to Controlling that leads resulting in balances being left on the project in any additional valuations (ledger 2L, here) and an incorrect value on the receiver in the second ledger. With Universal Parallel Accounting, SAP introduces a pure ledger approach where the two sets of values are handled completely independently and the values on the project are cleared properly and the assets under construction are capitalized correctly in each ledger

\

Conclusion

Parallel Accounting is an enabling capability to perform valuations and closing preparations according to the accounting principles. This key enabler provides a comparative data reporting architecture that can either be based on dedicated ledgers or dedicated general ledger accounts within one ledger.

 

 

SAP ERP add-on for financial accounting and operations SAP S/4HANA Finance SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance SAP Asset Strategy and Performance Management FIN Cost Object Controlling FIN Accounts Receivable and Payable   FIN Controlling FIN Cost Object Controlling SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting FIN Asset Accounting FIN Treasury SAP Treasury and Risk Management Treasury Management SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Asset Management SAP Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable SAP S/4HANA Finance for group reporting SAP Group Reporting Data Collection SD (Sales and Distribution) FIN Material Ledger MM (Materials Management) SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Sourcing and Procurement SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance SAP Receivables Manager 

@DataTherapist, @Laura Schmitz @Christoph Christophersen

entry.title: Building custom Excel view in SAPUI5 : Technical Part 1
entry.content:
I am starting a small blog series on how to build an excel editor. This might help if you are learning freestyle UI5 development and working with Files.

When working with Excel and dynamic table creation, you cannot hardcode  names and number of columns ( Headers ). Here in this sample application, both header and data are set dynamically.

I had came accross a really good blog for the same purpose and I have used the code as well. 

here is the link for the blog. https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/creation-of-dynamic-table-with-csv-xlsx-files-using-file-upload-in-sap-ui5/ba-p/13478701 by  poornamamatha

 

Consider my blog as extension of the above blog.

This is part 1 of the blog series and focus of this blog will be on a free style sample UI5 app with XLSX file upload with multiple worksheets.

I will cover advanced features in upcoming blogs.

 

Step 1 : A View that look similar to Excel

Initially, below code would be there in View1 ( or other name you give while generating app with fiori generator tools ). In below case, I set it to Home.view.xml 

 

 

 

 

<mvc:View controllerName="your-namespace.controller.Home"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc" displayBlock="true"
    xmlns="sap.m">
    <Page id="page" title="{i18n>title}">
        <content />
    </Page>
</mvc:View>

 

 

 

 

 

Replace above code with below code.

 

 

 

 

<mvc:View controllerName="xl.sx.excelxlsx.controller.Home"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:mvc="sap.ui.core.mvc"
    xmlns:unified="sap.ui.unified"
    xmlns:uitab="sap.ui.table"
    xmlns:l="sap.ui.layout"
    xmlns:f="sap.f"
    xmlns:c="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns:table="sap.ui.table">
    <f:DynamicPage id="dynamicPageId" 
                   stickySubheaderProvider="iconTabBar"
                   class="sapUiNoContentPadding">
        <!-- DynamicPage Title -->
        <!-- <f:title>
            <f:DynamicPageTitle>
                <f:heading>
                    <Title text="{i18n>MainPageTitle}" />
                </f:heading>
                <f:content>
                    <Toolbar>
                    </Toolbar>
                </f:content>
                <f:actions>
                </f:actions>
                <f:navigationActions>
				</f:navigationActions>
            </f:DynamicPageTitle>
        </f:title> -->

        <!-- DynamicPage Header -->
        <f:header>
            <f:DynamicPageHeader pinnable="true">
                <IconTabBar >
                    <items>
                        <IconTabFilter text="File">
                            <items>
                                <IconTabFilter text="Save"/>
                            </items>
                            <content>
                                <Button text="hi"></Button>
                            </content>
                        </IconTabFilter>
                        <IconTabFilter text="Home"></IconTabFilter>
                        <IconTabFilter text="Insert"></IconTabFilter>
                        <IconTabFilter text="Page Layout"></IconTabFilter>
                        <IconTabFilter text="Formulas"></IconTabFilter>
                        <IconTabFilter text="Data"></IconTabFilter>
                        <IconTabFilter text="Review"></IconTabFilter>
                        <IconTabFilter text="View"></IconTabFilter>
                        <IconTabFilter text="Help"></IconTabFilter>
                    </items>
                </IconTabBar>
            </f:DynamicPageHeader>
        </f:header>


        <!-- DynamicPage Content -->
        <f:content>
            <VBox>
              <OverflowToolbar id="otbSubheader1" 
                                backgroundDesign="Solid">
                <ToolbarSpacer />
                <unified:FileUploader 
                     id="FileUploaderid1" 
                     name="file" 
                     fileType="xlsx,csv" 
                     buttonOnly="true" 
                     change="onFileChange" 
                     icon="sap-icon://attachment-e-pub" />
                <Text text="/" class="sapUiTinyMargin" />
                <Button id="DBConnect1" 
                     backgroundDesign="Solid" 
                     press="onDBConnect" 
                     icon="sap-icon://disconnected" 
                     text="Connect to DB" />
               </OverflowToolbar>
               <IconTabBar id="id_itb">
               <items>
                 <IconTabFilter text="worksheet1">
                    <ScrollContainer height="500px" vertical="true" 
                                    horizontal="true" id="id_vbox1" 
                                    backgroundDesign="Solid">
                        <Table fixedLayout="Strict" 
                              backgroundDesign="Solid" 
                              showNoData="true" 
                              noDataText="{i18n>noSourceDataText}" 
                              id="TableId1" 
                              growingThreshold="10000" 
                              growingScrollToLoad="true" 
                              growing="true">
                        </Table>
                    </ScrollContainer>
                 </IconTabFilter>
               </items>
             </IconTabBar>
           </VBox>
        </f:content>
    </f:DynamicPage>
</mvc:View>

 

 

 

 

 

Add a Custom Css Class 

 

 

 

 

/* Enter your custom styles here */
.sapMListTblCellNoData {
    height: 400px;
}

 

 

 

 

When there is no data, it shows only one line with text set in "noData" property. for better view, height is set to 400 px even when there is no data.

 

After that, your screen would look like this..

 

Digression over File Uploader

Focus on below code snippet of FileUploader, it has gets rendered as button as buttonOnly is set to true.

When user clicks on the button, it opens up a popup for file selection. 

Allowed file types are set to XLSX and CSV.

 

 

 

 

<unified:FileUploader 
        id="FileUploaderid1" 
        name="file" 
        fileType="xlsx,csv" 
        buttonOnly="true"
        change="onFileChange" 
        icon="sap-icon://attachment-e-pub"  />

 

 

 

 

Another option is for connecting to any backend and get JSON data to populate in the table. Not required right now.

 

Step 2  : Add external Libraries

To work with XLSX file, we need to add dependencies of external libraries that can parse the file.

Add sheetJS library in init method of Component.js file. ( Also, JSZip is required as it is a dependency for sheetJS )

 

 

 

 

    var jQueryScript = document.createElement('script');
    jQueryScript.setAttribute('src', 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/xlsx/0.10.0/jszip.js');
    document.head.appendChild(jQueryScript);


    var jQueryScript = document.createElement('script');
    jQueryScript.setAttribute('src', 'https://cdnjs.cloudflare.com/ajax/libs/xlsx/0.10.0/xlsx.full.min.js');
    document.head.appendChild(jQueryScript);

 

 

 

 

 

your component.js file should look like this..

 

 

Step 3 : 

Initialize JSON Models 

 

 

 

onInit: function () {
    that = this;
    that.getView().setModel(new JSONModel(), "header");
    var oModelData1 = new JSONModel();
    oModelData1.setSizeLimit(1000);
    that.getView().setModel(oModelData1, "data");
},

 

 

 

Note : "that" is a variable declared globally in the controller and assigned to "this" in onInit method. 

Default JSON model size is 100, I have increased it to 1000. 

 

Handler for File Change event

Place below asynchronous function in your controller file.

Reading file is asynchronous activity and hence below function is declared as async. 

 

 

 

 

onFileChange: async function (oEvent) {
	var oFileUploader = that.getView().byId("FileUploaderid1");
	var oFile = oFileUploader.getFocusDomRef().files[0];
        that.oFile = oFile; // store for global usage
	
	// -ignore
	try {
		if (oFile.type === "application/vnd.ms-excel") {
			await that.typeCsv();
		}
		else {
			await that.typeXLSXwithTabs();
		}
		that.generateTablewithTabsxlsx();
	} catch (error) {
		console.log(error);
	}
},

 

 

 

 

 

Read file based on type of the file -  XLSX 

 

 

 

 

typeXLSXwithTabs: async function () {

    var file = that.file;
    var excelData = {};

    return new Promise((resolve, reject) => {
        if (file && window.FileReader) {
            var reader = new FileReader();
            reader.onload = async function (evt) {
                var data = evt.target.result;
                var workbook = XLSX.read(data, {
                    type: 'binary'
                });

                // loop over all worksheets of the uploaded XLSX File
                // and store them in excelData Array
                workbook.SheetNames.forEach(function (sheetName) {
                    excelData[sheetName] = XLSX.utils.sheet_to_row_object_array(workbook.Sheets[sheetName]);
                });


                that.getView().getModel("data").setData(excelData);
                that.getView().getModel("data").refresh(true);
                resolve(true);
            };
            reader.onerror = function (ex) {
                reject(false);
            };
            reader.readAsBinaryString(file);
        }
    });

},

 

 

 

 

 

 

Generate Tables dynamically based on tabs ( worksheets )

 

 

 

 

 

generateTablewithTabsxlsx: function () {

    var oITB = that.getView().byId("id_itb");
    oITB.destroyItems();


    var oModel = that.getView().getModel("data");
    var aTabs = Object.keys(oModel.getProperty("/"));
    var oTable;

    // loop over all worksheets stored in below array
    // for each worksheet => add a table
    //                       for each table => add columns and Cells
    $.each(aTabs, function (i, val) {
        oTable = new sap.m.Table({
            fixedLayout: false,
            backgroundDesign: sap.m.BackgroundDesign.Solid,
            showNoData: true
        });
        var oModelData = oModel.getProperty("/" + val + "/");
        var aColumns = Object.keys(oModelData[0]);
        var oColumnNames = [];
        $.each(aColumns, function (i, value) {
            oColumnNames.push({
                Text: aColumns[i]
            });
        });
        var columnmodel = that.getView().getModel("header");
        columnmodel.setProperty("/" + val + "/", oColumnNames);
        var oTemplate = new Column({
            header: new Label({
                text: "{header>Text}",
                customData: [
                    new sap.ui.core.CustomData({
                        key: "colName",
                        value: "{header>Text}",
                        writeToDom: true
                    })
                ],
            })
        });

        oTable.bindAggregation("columns", "header>/" + val + "/", oTemplate);
        var oItemTemplate = new ColumnListItem();


        $.each(aColumns, function (j, value) {
            var oHeaderName = value;
            oItemTemplate.addCell(new Text({
                text: "{data>" + oHeaderName + "}"
            }));
        });
        oTable.bindAggregation("items", {
            path: "data>/" + val + "/",
            template: oItemTemplate
        });


        // Add IconTabFilter for the current worksheet 
        // add the table, created in current loop, as content of IconTabFilter 
        oITB.addItem(
            new sap.m.IconTabFilter({
                text: val,
                content: new sap.m.ScrollContainer({
                    height: "350px",
                    vertical: true,
                    content: oTable
                })
            })
        );

    });

},

 

 

 

 

 

 

After uploading a file with single worksheet, it would look like ..

 

If there are more than one tabs ( worksheets ), it should look like ..

Worksheet 1 : Employee Data rendered as Icon Tab Filter with Content set as a Table

 

Worksheet 2 : Department Data rendered as Icon Tab Filter with Content set as a Table

 

Thanks you for going through the entire blog and I hope it may help in learning UI5 or developing any project.

Wait for upcoming blogs on more enhancements to this application.

 

 

`

entry.title: SAP ABAP 7.4 New Syntax for Inline Declaration
entry.content:
After version 7.4 in SAP, new syntax where introduced which increases the efficiency of the code written and also makes it easy for developers to code.  

Before 7.4 New syntaxes were not introduced the variable needs to be declared before using it, now using inline declaration variable can we declare and use at the same time. Below are some of the new syntaxes which has changed after 7.4.

Whenever data is to be moved to a variable, can be declared at the same instance:

Old Syntax.
DATA var1 TYPE char5.
var1 = ‘ABC’.

New Syntax.
DATA(var1) = ‘ABC’.

Select Single from Database Table into Work Area:

Old Syntax.
DATA wa TYPE ....
SELECT SINGLE fld1 fld2 FROM ....
INTO wa
WHERE fld1 = var1
AND fld2 = var2.

New Syntax.
SELECT SINGLE fld1, fld2, FROM ....
INTO (wa)
WHERE fld1 =
AND fld2 = @var2.

Selecting Data from Database Table into Internal Table:

Old Syntax.
DATA itab TYPE TABLE OF ....
SELECT fld1 fld2 FROM ....
INTO TABLE itab
WHERE fld1 = var1
AND fld2 = var2.

New Syntax.
SELECT fld1, fld2 FROM ....
INTO TABLE (itab)
WHERE fld1 =
AND fld2 = @var2. 

Declaration of the work area while operation on Internal Table:  

Old Syntax.
DATA wa TYPE structure
LOOP AT itab INTO wa.
or
READ TABLE itab into wa WITH KEY field1 = var1.

New Syntax.
LOOP AT itab INTO data(wa).
or
READ TABLE itab into data(wa) WITH KEY field1 = var1. 

Moving Data from One Table to Other:

Old Syntax.
DATA lt_tab LIKE itab.
lt_tab[] = itab[].

New Syntax.
DATA(lt_tab) = itab[]. 

Assigning Field Symbols:  

Old Syntax.
FIELD-SYMBOLS: <line> type …
LOOP AT itab ASSIGNING <line>.
or
READ TABLE itab ASSIGNING <line> WITH KEY FLD1 = VAR1.

New Syntax.
LOOP AT itab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line>).
OR
READ TABLE itab ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<line>) WITH KEY FLD1 = VAR1.

entry.title: Re: How to load the data to non prod HDI container tables...
entry.content:
If you want to copy all data from the source HDI container to target HDI container, you can use HDI container export/import via cloud storage. For details you may see this nice blog here: Export/Import an SAP HDI Container for Copy Purpos... - SAP Community
The HANA DB Explorer offers several ways to export and import data, the complete HDI container export/import is just one option.  
If you you want to copy specific tables/data only, you could alos utilize SDA to copy data from source to the target instance.

Regard, 
  Alexander

entry.title: Re: SD downpayment amount should include tax value
entry.content: Hello, from a business perspective DP normally do not carry tax values. Also be aware that in case you use tax integration such as VERTEX for US, downpayment will not trigger tax calculation to external tax rate engine. DP with Tax is not best practice. Greetings, Yves

entry.title: Profit Center in sales order is not saved
entry.content:
Good morning,

In the material master, I have set the profit center. However, when I create a sales order with this material, the profit center is not filled.

In the sales order I get the message that the sales order is not complete and that the profit center is missing.

I can enter the profit center under Account Assignment on item level. I get no further error messages and then I get the message that the document is complete:

However, when I save it, the entry in profit center is deleted again, and I get the message that the document is incomplete:

What is happening here? Why can't I save the profit center in the sales order? I have created several other sales orders for this sales organization, where I used the same profit center and that caused no problems.

Kind regards,

Floyd

entry.title: Flexible workflow for purchase requisition not triggered.
entry.content:
I have set all configurations of flexible workflow for purchase requisition but I have the following problem:

-After saving the PR, instead of the release strategy tab at item-wise approval, there is a tab "approval details". Having this tab shows that the system has realized that instead of the traditional release strategy, there should be workflow approval. But the issue is that this tab is all empty and no workflow has been determined. Previously I configured flexible workflow in another server (although I had some problems there) and in that server I would see that flexible workflow was at least triggered in the approval details tab.

Can anyone help me on this issue?
